_comment: Editable config for Layer 3 v1 (Fever Panel). Source = PharmEasy's own lab diagnostics. Two metrics shown year-over-year (this monsoon season vs last): (1) TESTS BOOKED -- overall Fever Panel demand, one line; (2) POSITIVITY -- the share of those tests coming back positive, broken down by disease. This REPLACES the old IDSP 'Confirmed Cases' layer (kept commented-out and switchable in index.html / build_site.py / the workflows). The current data is a MOCK (is_sample=true, status=coming_soon): src/build_layer3_panel.py with the 'mock' provider writes deterministic synthetic numbers, clearly badged as sample, until the real weekly backend feed is wired. Change metrics/diseases/window freely; no code edits needed.
_source_comment: Active provider. 'mock' (default) writes deterministic sample data. 'googlesheet' (stub, not yet live) will read the weekly backend export for the this_year series -- see the google_sheet block. Override with --provider or PANEL_SOURCE.

season._comment: Year-over-year window. last_year is the full historical reference; this_year fills in week by week from the backend. start_month..end_month bound the monsoon window so this layer lines up with Layers 1 and 2 for the eventual three-layer backtest.

_metrics_comment: The two trends. `kind` drives the chart y-axis + tick format: 'count' (tests booked, k-suffixed) or 'percent' (positivity, %). `headline` is the metric shown first. Positivity is split by disease; tests booked is the overall panel total (one line).

metrics.label: Tests booked
metrics.blurb: How many Fever Panel tests people booked, week by week. A demand signal.

metrics.label: Positivity
metrics.blurb: Of the panels run, the share that came back positive for each disease. A confirmation signal.

_diseases_comment: Diseases the Fever Panel reports positivity for. Dengue, Malaria, Chikungunya match the rest of the dashboard; Typhoid is a standard febrile-panel component. `headline` is the default-selected disease.

google_sheet._comment: NOT YET WIRED. When the backend publishes the weekly this_year feed as a Google Sheet, set csv_url to its published-to-web CSV link (File -> Share -> Publish to web -> CSV) and switch source to 'googlesheet'. The stub provider (src/panel_providers/googlesheet.py) reads that CSV, maps the columns below, and supplies the this_year series; last_year stays the committed historical reference. Stdlib only (urllib + csv); no new dependency.

sanity._comment: Fail-loud guard rails (same philosophy as Layers 1 and 2). The build aborts and writes nothing if the tests series is missing, if any tracked disease has no positivity series, or if a series has fewer than min_points_per_series points. A broken feed must never silently feed a branded dashboard.
